How I work

The process
that delivers
results.

From discovery to handoff — every step intentional, in close collaboration with stakeholders and developers.

01

Discovery

In-depth stakeholder and user interviews. Benchmarking against market leaders. Identifying real bottlenecks and opportunities.

02

Definition

Mapping critical flows, low-fidelity wireframes and clear requirement prioritisation with the team. Fast, iterative, always validated.

03

Design System

Reusable components, design tokens and full Figma documentation. High-fidelity interface — cohesive, accessible and scalable.

04

Validation & Handoff

Stakeholder testing, interactive prototypes and detailed documentation for developers. Post-launch follow-up with real metrics.

© 2025 Rodrigo Cafiero. All rights reserved.Porto, Portugal. Worldwide
SaaS · CRM · Design System · UX/UI · 2025

Vocalmeet
CRM Redesign

Full redesign of a SaaS CRM platform — scalable Design System, radical user flow optimisation and a modern cohesive UI from discovery to handoff.

UX/UI Design, Prototyping, Design System, Handoff
Figma
2025

Overview

The challenge

The Vocalmeet platform had long, fragmented flows creating unnecessary friction and slowing down task completion. Navigation between sections felt cumbersome — users needed many clicks to reach their goals. The interface was also outdated and inconsistent due to the absence of a unified Design System.

My objective was to streamline the experience by reducing steps, optimising navigation and introducing a modern, cohesive UI built on a scalable, reusable design foundation.

Process

How it was solved

01 · Discovery

In-depth conversations with the CEO and development team. Benchmarks against Stripe, Pipedrive and Relevance AI to define guiding UX principles.

02 · Definition

Mapped critical user flows and set clear goals: consolidate two sidebars into one, visual redesign, expanded data fields and click reduction.

03 · Design System

Complete Design System in Figma with reusable components, design tokens and detailed documentation to align design with development.

04 · Prototyping & Handoff

High-fidelity designs and interactive prototypes tested with stakeholders, delivered with full implementation documentation for the dev team.

Visual Impact

Before & after

Screenshots from the Vocalmeet CRM highlight improvements in layout, colour usage and simplified flows. Key interactions now allow faster task completion with less cognitive load.

CRM screens — redesigned navigation, contacts and dashboard views

Design System

A scalable foundation

The Design System was built in Figma from scratch, covering colour tokens, typography scale, spacing system, component library and interaction documentation — ensuring every future screen stays consistent.

Colour system and typography scale — part of the Vocalmeet Design System

Key screens

Contacts & dashboard

Results

Measurable impact

−70%Click reduction in main flows
1 DSComplete documented Design System
✓Positive feedback on clarity & usability

The new design delivered a more modern, intuitive and scalable experience. The Design System improved developer efficiency and visual consistency across the entire platform.

Next project

TIM Smart Briefing →

© 2025 Rodrigo Cafiero.Porto, Portugal. Worldwide
Telecom · UI/UX Design · Design System · 2024

TIM Smart
Briefing

Centralised platform optimising the briefing workflow for design, marketing and communication projects — from request to delivery, all in one place.

UX Research, UI Design, Design System, Component Library
Figma
2024

Overview

The problem

The TIM Smart Briefing is a UI/UX project aimed at optimising the briefing workflow for design, marketing and communication projects. The goal was to create an intuitive and efficient platform to capture essential information about project requests, ensuring that teams receive clear and well-structured instructions.

The process was fragmented — teams relied on scattered emails, spreadsheets and verbal requests. This created rework, communication failures and misaligned deliverables across departments.

TIM Smart Briefing — full platform overview across all screens

New initiative form on MacBook · Initiatives dashboard on tablet

Process

The process

01 · Stakeholder Research

Conducted in-depth interviews with project managers, designers and marketers to understand workflow bottlenecks, uncover pain points and define functional requirements for an optimised briefing system.

02 · User Flow & Wireframing

Mapped user journeys to eliminate inefficiencies, designed low-fidelity wireframes to validate concepts and iterated based on user feedback to refine the experience at every stage.

03 · UI/UX Design

Developed a clean, accessible and visually cohesive interface, ensuring a seamless user experience across different devices and user roles — from requestors to creative teams and managers.

04 · Design System & Handoff

Created a scalable design system with reusable components, establishing UI consistency, reducing design-developer handoff friction and improving future scalability across TIM Brasil's digital products.

Key screens

Initiatives dashboard

The home screen gives teams a real-time overview of all active initiatives — with filters by budget origin, classification and status. Cards show key data at a glance: macroavaliação, creation date, end date and time open.

Initiatives home — card view with status badges and project overview

List view & data management

An alternative list view enables power users to scan and manage large volumes of initiatives efficiently — with sortable columns for status, budget origin, macroavaliação, valoração, dates and time open.

Initiatives — list view with column sorting and action controls

Budget prioritisation

The prioritisation module allows teams to allocate budget across initiatives using a drag-and-drop interface — with real-time budget total and consumed tracking to support data-driven decisions.

Budget prioritisation — drag-and-drop allocation with total and consumed budget tracking

Key Features

Built for collaboration

  • Dynamic and interactive forms — structured data collection tailored to each type of project request, reducing ambiguity and rework
  • Integration with management tools — connected to Trello, Asana and Monday.com for seamless task creation from briefings
  • Centralised database — complete history of briefings and projects accessible by all stakeholders in real time
  • Budget prioritisation module — visual drag-and-drop interface for allocating and managing initiative budgets across teams
  • User-friendly interface — designed for different user profiles, from creative teams to executives, with role-based navigation
  • Responsive design — full functionality across desktop, tablet and mobile for field and remote teams

Results & Impact

Measurable impact

−80%Rework from incomplete or unclear briefings
1 hubSingle platform replacing emails and spreadsheets
3×Faster project opening and stakeholder alignment

By implementing these strategies, I helped create a standardised, automated and collaborative briefing process that streamlines project requests and improves team efficiency across TIM Brasil's marketing and design operations.

Vale App Checklist →

© 2025 Rodrigo Cafiero.Porto, Portugal. Worldwide
Enterprise · Mobile · Field Operations · 2024

Vale — Digital
Inspection App

Mobile application digitising vehicle and equipment mobilisation checklists at Vale — replacing paper forms with an offline-ready, MEV-integrated solution.

User Flow, UI/UX Design, Mobile
Figma
2024

Overview

The challenge

The Vale App Checklist project was designed to digitise and streamline the vehicle and equipment mobilisation checklist process. The current process was entirely manual — printed forms filled out in the field, scanned and sent via email — creating data integrity issues and delays.

The goal was to create a mobile application enabling digital checklists, even offline, storing data systematically and integrating with the MEV system used across Vale's operations.

Vale App Checklist — mobile interface for field operations

Features

Built for the field

Digital Checklists

Replaced manual paper forms with structured digital checklists — faster, consistent and always accessible on mobile devices.

Offline Mode

Designed for remote industrial environments without internet access. Data is stored locally and synchronised with MEV when back online.

MEV Integration

Seamless data synchronisation with Vale's internal MEV management system, eliminating manual re-entry and reducing errors.

Accessibility First

Interface optimised for field workers — larger touch targets, high contrast, and usability validated for older mobile devices used in industrial environments.

My contributions

Design decisions

  • User Flow & Wireframing — Designed streamlined flows to simplify data entry and validation for field workers
  • Mobile-first UI — Interface built for industrial conditions: glare, gloves, time pressure and older devices
  • Offline-first architecture — UX patterns that work seamlessly regardless of connectivity
  • Accessibility — WCAG-aligned touch targets, contrast ratios and navigation patterns

Results

Measurable impact

0Paper forms — fully digital process
✓Works offline in remote areas
↑Data accuracy & operational efficiency

By implementing these strategies, the manual, error-prone process was transformed into a digital, efficient and scalable solution — improving operational efficiency and data integrity across Vale's field operations.

Next project

Vale Pelotization →

© 2025 Rodrigo Cafiero.Porto, Portugal. Worldwide
Enterprise · Industrial UX · Design System · 2024

Vale
Pelotization

Designing clarity in complex industrial operations — a unified platform transforming fragmented workflows into a cohesive, decision-driven experience.

User Flow, UI/UX Design, Component Library
Figma
2024

Context

The challenge

Industrial environments don't fail because of lack of data. They fail because data is hard to interpret, fragmented, and slow to act on.

The pelletisation workflow at Vale relied on a disconnected ecosystem — Excel spreadsheets, Power Apps, Power BI dashboards and email threads. The result was high operational friction and low decision velocity.

Problems identified

Teams couldn't track project progress in real time. Inconsistency across tools. Difficulty collaborating across departments. Reports consolidated manually — slow and error-prone.

The core gap

The system wasn't designed for decision-making — it was designed for data storage. This created a gap between information availability and actionability.

Vale Integrated Pelletization System — Portal overview across all modules

Strategy

The opportunity

How might we transform fragmented data into a clear, actionable system that supports fast and confident decision-making?

Instead of simply "centralising tools," the focus was on designing for decisions — not data. Three strategic principles guided the work:

01 · Design for decisions

Surface what matters most — when it matters. Every screen was designed around the question: what does this user need to decide right now?

02 · Reduce cognitive load

Simplify how users process complex information. Strong visual hierarchy, structured layouts and functional colour use allow fast scanning of dense datasets.

03 · Align with real workflows

Design around how teams actually operate — not how systems are structured. The shift: from tool-based navigation to task-based experience.

04 · Build to scale

A scalable component library and consistent interaction patterns ensure the system can grow with the product — new modules, new data sources, new teams.

Process

How it was solved

  • Mapping complexity — Analysed the full lifecycle of pelletisation projects to identify breakpoints in information flow, redundant manual processes and moments of high cognitive load
  • Reframing the system — Shifted from tool-based navigation to task-based experience: instead of "which tool should I use?" the system answers "what do you need to do right now?"
  • User Flow & Wireframing — Designed workflows to improve project monitoring and eliminate inefficiencies, with low-fidelity wireframes validated with stakeholders before visual design
  • Structuring information — Grouped data by relevance, not source. Prioritised critical data visibility and reduced unnecessary steps and interactions
  • UI/UX Design — Clean, structured layouts for complex datasets with a strong visual hierarchy for fast scanning and functional use of colour to indicate status and priority
  • Component Library — Reusable components and consistent interaction patterns as the foundation for future features and scalability

Key screens

Project budget & detailed data

The budget screen allows teams to manage detailed financial information per project phase — tabs for FEL 1, FEL 2, FEL 3, Detailed and Implementation. Export and import tools streamline data management workflows without leaving the platform.

Project budget management — phase tabs, financial fields and import/export controls

Data imports & administration

The administration area centralises imports from multiple external sources — App Maturity, Demandas, Ferramenta de Captura, Portfólio and SAP — giving teams full control over data integrity and update history in a single organised view.

Data imports administration — centralised management of all external data sources

Supply plans & packages

The supply plan module enables teams to search, view and manage procurement plans — with filters by code, PEP, process type, discipline, site and status. Real-time data across the integrated portal eliminates the need for external spreadsheets.

Supply plans — procurement package management on tablet with Apple Pencil

Document tracking & management

A searchable, filterable document list with status tracking (Em andamento, Pendente, Concluído), bulk editing and direct GED integration — replacing manual document consolidation with a structured, always up-to-date view.

Document listing — full-text search, status badges, GED integration and bulk operations

Responsive across all devices

Designed to work seamlessly across desktop and tablet — enabling field engineers and project managers to access and update information from any device, in any environment, without losing context or functionality.

Collaborator registration and budget management — responsive experience on tablet devices

Results & Impact

Measurable impact

1Unified platform replacing 4+ fragmented tools
↓Reduced time to access critical project information
↑Faster and more confident decision-making

Operational value delivered

Increased clarity across project stages. Lower cognitive effort when analysing data. Improved alignment between teams. Reduced manual consolidation of reports.

Key insight

Complexity is not the problem — unmanaged complexity is. By structuring information around user intent and decision points, the system became easier to understand, faster to use and more effective in real-world scenarios.

The system shifts from being a passive data repository to an active decision-support tool — transforming fragmented, manual workflows into a streamlined, data-driven solution that improves project oversight and operational efficiency across Vale's pelletisation operations.

Energy · Design System · WCAG Accessibility · 2024

Eletrobras
Design System

Scalable, accessible Design System unifying the UI across multiple platforms of Latin America's largest energy company.

Design System Lead, UI/UX Design
Figma, React / Angular
2024

About the project

As part of Eletrobras' digital transformation, I developed a Design System to unify and standardise the UI across various platforms — creating a scalable, accessible and efficient system to improve consistency and streamline design-development processes.

Challenges & solutions

Multi-team adoption

Clear documentation, internal workshops and highly intuitive components reduced the learning curve across multiple teams.

Brand identity

Balanced UI modernisation with preservation of Eletrobras' established visual identity.

WCAG Accessibility

All components designed and tested to WCAG 2.1 AA — contrast, keyboard navigation and screen reader support.

Frontend integration

Close collaboration with dev teams for React and Angular integration, ensuring Figma components matched real implementation.

Results & impact

AAWCAG 2.1 compliance across all components
↑UI consistency across multiple platforms
↓Reduced development time per feature

Impar Design System →

© 2025 Rodrigo Cafiero.Porto, Portugal. Worldwide
Tech · Design System · Digital Workplace · 2023

Impar
Design System

Full Design System for Impar's Digital Workplace platform — cutting delivery time from 2 weeks to 2 days.

Design System, UI/UX, Accessibility
Figma
2023

The problem

Internal digital tools were developed independently, resulting in inconsistent UI components, redundant work for designers and developers, limited accessibility compliance and a fragmented user experience reducing employee productivity. The deadline was just two weeks to deliver the full Digital Workplace.

The process

1 — Research & Audit

Assessment of existing UI inconsistencies and stakeholder insights to understand real platform needs.

2 — Design & Prototyping

Reusable components in Figma ensuring modularity and scalability — built to grow with the product.

3 — Documentation

Best practices for usage, accessibility and design consistency — facilitating team-wide adoption.

4 — Implementation & Handoff

Close collaboration with developers to ensure faithful component integration in the final product.

Results & impact

2dDelivery time (vs. 2 weeks before)
↓Significant rework and inconsistency reduction
↑Team productivity on strategic problems

Agibank serves a broad demographic including unbanked and underbanked users. The redesign focused on simplifying complex financial flows and creating an experience that works for users with varying levels of digital literacy — while maintaining a modern, trustworthy aesthetic.

Design focus areas

Onboarding simplification

Streamlined account opening flow reducing drop-off points and making the process accessible to first-time digital banking users.

Financial inclusion

Designed for users with varying digital literacy — clear language, guided interactions and accessibility-first components.

Transaction clarity

Improved information hierarchy in transaction screens, making it easier to understand balances, limits and payment history.

Trust & security signals

Visual design choices that reinforce trustworthiness and security — critical for financial product adoption.

EBAC Games →

© 2025 Rodrigo Cafiero.Porto, Portugal. Worldwide
Gaming · Mobile · UX Design · 2022

EBAC Games —
Mobile Gaming

Digital platform giving visibility to indie mobile game studios — intuitive discovery, organised catalogue and a smooth purchase flow for players and creators.

UX Design, Research, Prototyping
Figma
2022

Overview

About the project

EBAC Games is a digital platform focused on indie mobile games, with the mission of giving visibility to small studios and independent developers — promoting unique and creative experiences in the gaming world.

The main goal was to design an intuitive, accessible and engaging digital experience that supports both players and game creators. We developed a website that emphasises usability, a well-organised game catalogue and a smooth, secure purchasing flow.

EBAC Games — platform overview showing game catalogue and creator tools

Process

Research to design

Through research, benchmarking, user personas and usability testing, we structured a user journey that prioritises the discovery, promotion and monetisation of indie games. Developers can manage their content with ease, while users enjoy a seamless browsing experience.

Research & Benchmarking

Competitive analysis of leading game platforms (Steam, itch.io, Apple Arcade). User personas for both players (discovery-focused) and creators (visibility and monetisation).

User Journey Mapping

Structured dual journeys: players browsing and purchasing games, and developers managing and monetising their content — both needing clear, different paths.

Usability Testing

Tested key flows — game discovery, purchase and developer content management — iterating based on real user feedback until smooth and intuitive.

Final Design

Visual design balancing the playful aesthetic of gaming culture with the clarity needed for a commerce and creator platform.

Key design decisions

Two users, one platform

  • Dual user architecture — clear, separate flows for players (browsing, discovery, purchase) and developers (upload, manage, analyse)
  • Game catalogue design — organised browsing with filters, categories and featured indie titles to maximise discoverability
  • Purchase flow — simplified checkout with clear pricing, secure payment indicators and instant download access
  • Developer dashboard — intuitive content management with upload tools, analytics and promotional options
  • Mobile-first approach — optimised for the primary device where indie mobile games are played and discovered

Results

Measurable impact

2User types served — players and creators
✓Purchase flow validated in usability testing
↑Indie game discoverability and creator tools
